data.title: Crude oil resumes slide as oversupply concerns subdue India news
data.description:
Oil futures extended losses Thursday, giving up early gains made after President Trump said India will in time stop buying oil from Russia, as market concerns about oversupply remain a burden on prices.

Trump said Wednesday that Prime Minister Modi had pledged that India eventually will stop buying from Russia - India's top oil supplier - but a statement from India's foreign ministry made no reference to Trump's comments, saying the country's policy remains one of broad-based energy sourcing and diversification, and that it is discussing deeper energy cooperation with the U.S.

The U.S. and India have held productive trade talks, and Indian refiners already have reduced Russian oil imports by 50%, a White House official told Reuters.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration reported domestic crude inventories rose by 3.5 million barrels to 423.8 million barrels last week, up for the third straight week and contrary to a Wall Street Journal survey that forecast a 1.1 million barrel draw in crude stocks, with refineries running at 85.7% of capacity, down from 92.4% the previous week, due to the start of fall turnarounds.

However, gasoline inventories dropped by 267,000 barrels to 218.8 million barrels last week, and distillate fuel stocks fell by 4.5 million barrels to 117 million barrels.

"A modestly bearish report, with a large crude build being offset by a large distillate draw, but with implied oil demand considerably weaker than last week," UBS analyst Giovanni Staunovo said in a note.

Also, President Trump said he will meet with Russia's President Putin in Budapest to discuss ending the war in Ukraine, raising the possibility that Russian crude may soon flow freely.

Russian exports of refined fuels have fallen to their lowest since the early days of the war, evidence of the strain on the country’s refineries targeted by Ukrainian drone attacks.

Energy traders also are concerned that rising trade disputes between the U.S. and China could weaken demand for petroleum products if tensions stay elevated.

Earlier this week, the International Energy Agency raised its forecast for a record surplus next year to 4 million bbl/day, as OPEC+ ramps up production.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery closed -1.4% to $57.46/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for December delivery ended -1.4% to $61.06/bbl, the fifth decline in the past six sessions for both benchmarks and the lowest settlement since May 5.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) fell for a third straight day the EIA reported a weekly storage build of 80 Bcf, in line with expectations, keeping inventories on track to start the winter at a surplus; the front-month Nymex November contract settled -2.6% at $2.938/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

data.title: United Airlines targets double-digit margin expansion through customer loyalty investments and cost efficiency
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (UAL) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Scott Kirby opened by stating United delivered strong third quarter results and now expects to grow earnings for the full year. He emphasized, "the brand loyal United next strategy is resilient in tough times and a clear proof point on our path to solid double-digit margins." Kirby highlighted over $1 billion in annual investments in customer product enhancements, covering all cabins, and noted, "every United customer simply gets more value at United than what our competitors offer." He described the company’s strategic shift: "United is doing the exact opposite of that industry dogma, namely investing more for the customer and focusing on flying at times that can be profitable."

  • Brett Hart, President, reported, "This summer was the busiest in United's history. We surpassed 1 billion available seat miles in a single day and flew over 48 million customers in the quarter." He underlined operational resilience despite summer weather and ATC challenges, with the "lowest rate of cancellations for any third quarter in company history." Hart discussed ongoing investments in technology and the plan to hire over 2,000 pilots and 3,200 new flight attendants in 2026.

  • Andrew Nocella, CCO, stated, "United's top line revenues increased 2.6% to $15.2 billion in the quarter on a 7.2% increase in capacity." He noted international TRASM down 7.1%, domestic TRASM down 3.3%, and premium revenues up 6% year-over-year. Nocella indicated strategic capacity adjustments for Q3 2026, including ending the summer schedule a week early and reducing redeye flights.

  • CFO Michael Leskinen stated, "We delivered third quarter earnings per share of $2.78 above the top end of our guidance range of $2.25 to $2.75." He highlighted a pretax margin of 8% and CASM-ex down 0.9%. Leskinen added, "We expect fourth quarter EPS to be $3 to $3.50, that brings our full year EPS towards the better half of our full year 2025 guidance range of $9 to $11 and should position us to be the only airline to grow earnings this year." He described continued cost efficiency from technology investments and headcount reductions.

Outlook

  • Management projects fourth quarter EPS of $3 to $3.50 and full year EPS toward the higher end of the $9 to $11 range. Leskinen stated, "We continue to target double-digit pretax margins in the long term."

  • Nocella outlined changes in summer 2026 capacity planning, such as ending the summer schedule a week early and reducing redeye flights by 15%. He expects Atlantic capacity, excluding Tel Aviv, to be flat to negative in Q3 2026.

  • Management plans to grow premium capacity 2 to 3 points more than total capacity in 2026.

Financial Results

  • United reported Q3 earnings per share of $2.78, pretax margin of 8%, and revenues of $15.2 billion. Premium revenues increased 6% year-over-year. CASM-ex was down 0.9%. Loyalty revenues grew over 9%, with overhead remuneration up 15% year-over-year.

  • Free cash flow generation is expected to exceed $3 billion in 2025. Leskinen stated, "We bought back 377 aircraft off of expensive COVID air leases" and eliminated all expensive financing from the balance sheet.

Q&A

  • Catherine O'Brien, Goldman Sachs: Asked about main cabin margin dynamics if there is a change in supply. Kirby responded that "brand loyal is higher margin, but commodity loses money," and the future will favor brand loyal carriers with more stable earnings and mid-teens margins.

  • Jamie Baker, JPMorgan: Inquired about premium leisure yields versus corporate yields. Nocella explained, "the quality of premium leisure business often exceeds that of traditional corporate business" on domestic routes.

  • Andrew Didora, BofA: Asked about Latin America growth. Nocella said, "Results for Latin were disappointed," and capacity in the region will be reduced where underperforming.

  • Sheila Kahyaoglu, Jefferies: Requested details on margin expansion drivers. Kirby stated, "I feel pretty confident that we ought to be able to get at least 1 point of margin per year," driven by gains among brand loyal customers and loyalty program growth.

  • Duane Pfennigwerth, Evercore ISI: Asked about cost shifts. Leskinen detailed maintenance and labor timing impacts and reaffirmed 2% to 3% CASM-ex growth over multiple years.

  • Questions also covered Starlink Wi-Fi installation cadence, fleet decisions, government shutdown impact, and competitive dynamics with low-cost carriers.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on cost containment, competitive positioning of premium versus main cabin, and sustainability of revenue gains, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarification on strategic execution and future drivers.

  • Management maintained a highly confident tone, using phrases such as "We are confident..." and "Our strategy is working," while frequently referencing structural advantages and long-term plans. The tone was more forward-looking and assertive compared to the previous quarter, which was more cautious amid macro uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Compared to Q2 2025, management shifted from discussing demand inflection and Newark recovery to emphasizing long-term double-digit margin targets and the resilience of the brand loyal strategy. Q3 saw an increase in operational and financial confidence, highlighted by higher premium revenue growth and more aggressive future capacity adjustments.

  • Analysts in both quarters focused on cost structure and margin drivers, but Q3 included more questions about sustainability of premium trends and future loyalty program expansion.

  • Management’s sentiment in Q3 was more upbeat, projecting margin growth and increased investment in customer experience, while Q2 remarks were more focused on overcoming short-term disruptions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing labor negotiations with flight attendants and the need for contract resolution. Hart noted, "We remain focused on getting our flight attendants, the best in the industry, the industry-leading contract they deserve."

  • Nocella acknowledged underperformance in Latin America and capacity adjustments planned for weaker routes.

  • Kirby mentioned geopolitical and regulatory risks, specifically overflight restrictions affecting China routes, and competitive disadvantage versus non-U.S. carriers.

  • Government shutdown risks were addressed, with Leskinen stating Q4 guidance includes room for shutdown impacts but not for multiple disruptive events.

Final Takeaway

United Airlines management projects ongoing margin expansion and enhanced earnings resilience, underpinned by continued investments in customer experience, technology-driven cost efficiency, and a growing base of brand loyal customers. Strategic adjustments in route planning and product offerings, alongside disciplined capital management and a focus on premium and loyalty revenues, are expected to drive long-term value for shareholders even in a volatile industry environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Snap-on outlines continued momentum with 8.9% organic RS&I growth and record Q3 EPS as diagnostics and OEM dealership gains accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Snap-on Incorporated (SNA) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Nicholas Pinchuk, Chairman, CEO & President, emphasized "encouraging momentum, continuing our progress, moving upward against one of the most challenging environments of our time, wars, inflation and tariffs" and described the quarter as "fortified by the progress along our runways for both growth and improvement." He highlighted third quarter sales of $1,190.8 million, up 3.8% from last year, with organic sales up 3%. He noted that the quarter's sales were "up sequentially over the second quarter results, again, against normal seasonality."
  • Pinchuk reported an OpCo operating income margin of 23.4%, including 190 basis points from a legal settlement, adjusting to 21.5%. He called out that EPS was $5.02, or $4.71 excluding the legal benefit—"the highest ever for a third quarter and overcoming a $0.09 impact from higher pension amortization costs."
  • The CEO underlined a "continuing need in a rising complexity" of the automotive repair market, referencing a car park now averaging "nearly 12.8 years." He described a pivot toward "faster payback items," noting that this strategy "is taking hold and it's making gains."
  • On product launches, Pinchuk cited the new TAC2 torque and angle click wrench and a new 14.4-volt 3-inch extra-long cordless ratchet, stating, "everybody is talking about it. It speeds up repair, and it's already a hit million product."
  • Regarding the Tools Group, Pinchuk described "sales volume up organically 1%, increased activity in the international network and slightly higher sales in the U.S." He highlighted the Snap-on Franchisee Conference with SFC orders "up nicely, increasing over last year by mid-single digits."
  • For RS&I, Pinchuk reported sales of $464.8 million, up 10% as reported, with organic improvement of 8.9%. He noted, "the new TRITON handheld had a strong quarter" and said OEM dealership sales "grew by double digits."
  • Aldo Pagliari, Senior VP of Finance & CFO, stated, "Net sales of $1,190.8 million in the quarter represented an increase of 3.8% from 2024 levels, reflecting a 3% organic sales gain and $9 million of favorable foreign currency translation."

Outlook

  • Aldo Pagliari forecasted corporate costs to "approximate $27 million" for Q4. He stated, "we expect to incur approximately $6 million pretax in the fourth quarter of increased nonservice pension costs...will have about $0.09 per diluted share negative effect on EPS in the fourth quarter of 2025."
  • Management expects capital expenditures for the year will approximate $100 million and anticipates a full year 2025 effective income tax rate between 22% and 23%.
  • The fiscal year will include 53 weeks, with the additional week at the end of Q4, historically not having a significant effect on full-year or Q4 total revenues and net earnings.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated gross margin was 50.9%, up sequentially from 50.5% in Q2 and compared to 51.2% in the prior year's Q3. The year-over-year decline of 30 basis points was mainly due to unfavorable foreign currency effects.
  • Operating expenses as a percentage of net sales were 27.5%, improved from 29.2% last year, primarily due to a $22 million legal settlement benefit.
  • Operating earnings before Financial Services were $278.5 million, and operating margin before Financial Services was 23.4%, including the legal settlement benefit.
  • Financial services revenue was $101.1 million, with operating earnings of $68.9 million, down from $71.7 million last year. Total loan originations declined by 4.8% year-over-year.
  • Net earnings were $265.4 million, or $5.02 per diluted share, including a $16.2 million or $0.31 per diluted share after-tax benefit from the legal settlement.

Q&A

  • Christopher Glynn, Oppenheimer: Questioned consistency in RS&I growth. Pinchuk explained, "We feel kind of good about that. But we'll see how it goes going forward."
  • Glynn: Asked about undercar stabilization and OEM share. Pinchuk replied, "undercar looked like it stabilized this quarter...the OEM business is both programs happening and share gain."
  • Glynn: Asked about C&I in Europe. Pinchuk said, "the grassroots in Europe are starting to display the same kind of uncertainty or reticence that you see in the United States."
  • David MacGregor, Longbow: Inquired about sequential volume and margin outlook. Pinchuk attributed momentum to "more effective pivoting" and not SFC order timing.
  • MacGregor: Asked about pricing and mix. Pinchuk stated, "the margins were up pretty good...I don't think we're worried so much about that situation. Certainly, we don't think the pricing is eroding."
  • Scott Stember, ROTH: Sought Tools Group breakdown. Pinchuk said, "tool storage wasn't that strong...hand tools did not have a great quarter...Diagnostics was up big."
  • Stember: Asked about C&I and government funding. Pinchuk cited delays due to trade uncertainty: "anybody who's got factories...is sitting there saying, what hell do I do?"
  • Bret Jordan, Jefferies: Asked about off-the-truck tool sales trends. Pinchuk found "no notable trend in the quarter," citing ongoing technician uncertainty.
  • Jordan: Probed on pricing outlook for 2026. Pinchuk responded, "I don't see us necessarily raising our prices hugely unless things change."
  • Luke Junk, Baird: Asked about SFC order mix. Pinchuk responded, "nothing special about mix in this situation."
  • Junk: Queried on diagnostics growth. Pinchuk confirmed, "the Tools Group was every bit as good as that or more" than RS&I in diagnostics.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, with a focus on sustainability of growth in diagnostics, Tools Group mix, C&I government order delays, and pricing outlook. They pressed for clarity on margins, order patterns, and demand drivers but did not express marked skepticism.
  • Management's tone in prepared remarks was confident, using phrases like "we proceed with confidence" and "momentum, performance and new highs." During Q&A, management was open but cautious on forward pricing and order conversion, occasionally expressing the need to "see how it goes going forward."
  • Compared to last quarter, analysts' tone remained steady, while management displayed more optimism about sequential momentum and less focus on shock recovery.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sequentially, third quarter sales rose over Q2, reversing typical seasonality. Organic growth in Q3 was 3% versus a 0.7% decline in Q2.
  • RS&I organic growth accelerated to 8.9% from 2.3% in Q2, with particular strength in diagnostics and OEM dealership sales.
  • Tools Group maintained positive organic growth, with margin stability despite ongoing technician uncertainty and weak hand tools performance.
  • C&I Group improved sequentially, with operating margin up to 15.6% from 13.5% in Q2, driven by critical industries and specialty torque, despite Asia Pacific weakness.
  • Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism about recovery in Q2 to more assertive confidence in Q3, highlighting resilience and record profitability.
  • Analysts maintained focus on mix, sustainability of growth, and pricing, with no marked increase in skepticism.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited persistent uncertainty from global trade policies, tariffs, and geopolitical events, noting that "the rapid-fire policies out of Washington and the headwinds politically and economically across the globe have clouded the prospects."
  • Delays in government-funded and large industrial projects remain a challenge for C&I, with order activity increasing but timing uncertain.
  • Technician confidence remains low, leading to continued reluctance for big-ticket purchases, impacting loan originations and tool storage sales.
  • Management is monitoring supply chain disruptions and currency volatility, with mitigation strategies including short supply chains and flexible manufacturing.

Final Takeaway

Snap-on management emphasized that the third quarter delivered record Q3 EPS, sequential organic sales growth, and robust profitability despite persistent macroeconomic turbulence and currency headwinds. The company credited accelerating demand for diagnostics and OEM dealership solutions, successful product pivots, and expanding customer connections for driving ongoing momentum. Management expressed confidence in navigating future volatility, leveraging its manufacturing footprint and innovative product pipeline to support continued growth across its core markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: EMD Serono to offer discounted fertility treatments in TrumpRx IVF program
data.description:
EMD Serono, the U.S. healthcare business of Germany's Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF), has brokered an agreement with the Trump administration to provide its in vitro fertilization at a deep discount to US consumers through the TrumpRx prescription drug program.

The company will provide direct-to-consumer purchasing for IVF therapies, including Gonal-f (follitropin alfa injection), Ovidrel (choriogonadotropin alfa injection), and Cetrotide (cetrorelix acetate for injection).

EMD Serono said that when the three drugs are used in a typical IVP protocol, women will save 84% off list prices.

In a fact sheet, the White House said that CMS estimates that women will save $2,200 per cycle of fertility drugs as a result of the arrangement, adding that the medications often account for ~20% of a IVF treatment cycle's cost.

EMD Serono will also file Pergoveris (follitropin alfa and lutropin alfa), which is already approved in Europe, for review by the US FDA under the National Priority Voucher program which cuts review time to 1-2 months from the usual 10-12. The White House called Pergoveris "a lower-cost alternative to expensive medications already on the U.S. market."

The administration added that EMD Serono will begin manufacturing its IVF drugs in the US. As a result, the company will not face potential pharma tariffs.

The president also unveiled a new benefit option for employers to offer fertility benefits directly to employees.

Separately, CVS Health (CVS) said it will be a "core partner" in the IVF drugs program through its CVS Specialty Pharmacy for those with insurance coverage for medications by providing pre-verification, claims support, and financial assistance.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: NextDecade makes positive final investment decision on Rio Grande LNG Train 5
data.description:
NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) +3.9% post-market Thursday after saying it reached a positive final investment decision on Train 5 of its Rio Grande liquefied natural gas export project in Texas, slightly more than a month after moving forward with a fourth train at the export terminal.

NextDecade (NASDAQ:NEXT) also said the Train 5 FID came with a notice to proceed to Bechtel, the engineering, procurement and construction contractor for Rio Grande LNG.

Train 5 is commercially supported by 4.5 million metric tons/year of 20-year LNG agreements with JERA, EQT Corp., and ConocoPhillips; the planned date of the first commercial delivery from Train 5 is anticipated in H1 2031.

Train 5, which NextDecade (NEXT) expects will cost ~$6.7 billion to build, has expected LNG production capacity of ~6 million metric tons/year, bringing the total expected LNG production capacity under construction at Rio Grande LNG to ~30 million tons/year.

data.title: Rogers announces Armand Lauzon as new Chair of Board
data.description:
• Rogers (NYSE:ROG) announced on Thursday that Peter Wallace would be stepping down as the Chair of the Board. In anticipation, Armand Lauzon has been appointed as the next Chair effective immediately.

  • Armand Lauzon, with his expertise in strategy, operations, and governance and over 40 years of experience leading industrial and manufacturing companies through transformation and growth, would create long-term value for the shareholder.

  • The company also had plan to add a new independent director to the board. A director search is currently underway.

data.title: Gold hits another record with bank worries in focus alongside trade, rate cuts
data.description:
Gold and silver soared again to new all-time highs Thursday, sparked by concerns about credit quality in the economy, in addition to U.S.-China trade frictions and growing expectations for further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

Broader markets were hit as two major regional banks disclosed problems with loans involving allegations of fraud, adding to concerns about wider credit-related losses in the system, following the bankruptcies of two auto industry-related companies, which have helped boost demand for safe-havens such as gold and silver.

"When you see one cockroach, there are probably more," JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said on the bank’s earnings conference call earlier this week, referring to the collapse of First Brands and Tricolor Holdings.

Fed Chair Powell signaled this week that the Fed is on track to deliver another 25 basis point rate cut this month, and traders are betting on another reduction in December; non-yielding gold typically performs well in a low-rate environment.

Gold also has been supported by concerns over a resurgence in trade tensions, particularly between the U.S. and China.

The dollar continues to face headwinds of a stretched valuation and unsustainable U.S. fiscal policies, and investors seeking alternatives to the dollar are preferring gold over other developed market currencies, Insight Investment's Francesca Fornasari said in a note. which "likely reflects the challenging fiscal outlook for traditional safe-haven currencies like the Japanese yen and euro, as seen in France's budget issues" and concerns about the next leader of Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party.

"While comparisons are being made to the 1980s price peak, the current price rise is underpinned by structural drivers, indicating that elevated prices will likely sustain," according to analysts at Australian bank ANZ, which now forecast gold at $4,400/oz by year-end with a peak expected near $4,600/oz by June 2026.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed +2.5% to $4,280.20/oz, and front-month Comex October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +3.8% to $53.023/oz, the fifth consecutive daily increases for both metals that have seen gold gain 8.4% and silver surge 13.2%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Go Residential Reit refinances Manhattan property, adds $64.5 million liquidity
data.description:
• GO Residential Real Estate Investment Trust (GO.U) said on Thursday that it has refinanced its One East River Place property in Manhattan, raising the mortgage principal to $251 million from $186.5 million.

  • The new five-year term loan adds about $64.5 million in proceeds, which the REIT used to partially repay its credit facility, currently bearing interest at SOFR plus 1.75%.
  • The move is expected to improve adjusted funds from operations per unit and bolster financial flexibility, the company said.
  • The base portion of the mortgage retains its prior rate until mid-2027, while the incremental amount carries a 5.02% rate through maturity in September 2030.
  • GO Residential said the refinancing extends its debt maturity profile and lowers near-term refinancing risk.
  • GO.U:CA closed -1.65% at $11.85.
  • Source: Press Release
